meals.name: Sweet and Sour Chicken Stir Fry with Rice
meals.description: A quick, one-wok stir fry with tender chicken, bell peppers, pineapple, and a tangy homemade sauce, served over rice. Total cook time is approximately 35 minutes.
instructions: Cook white rice according to package directions. Set aside.
Cut chicken into 1-inch pieces. Chop bell peppers and onion into 1-inch pieces. Drain pineapple, reserving 2 tablespoons of juice.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, ketchup, corn starch, garlic powder, sesame oil, and reserved pineapple juice to make the sweet and sour sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until browned and cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add bell peppers and onion to the skillet and stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until slightly tender-crisp. Return chicken to the skillet, add pineapple chunks, and pour the sauce over everything.
Stir constantly until the sauce thickens and coats the chicken and vegetables, about 1-2 minutes. Serve immediately over the cooked white rice.

meals.name: Beef Tacos
meals.description: An easy, build-your-own taco night with seasoned ground beef and all the fixings. Fast, fun, and a guaranteed crowd-pleaser. Total cook time is approximately 25 minutes.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up, until browned, about 6-7 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Stir in the taco seasoning and 1/3 cup water. Simmer 3-4 minutes until thickened.
While the beef cooks, shred the lettuce, dice the tomatoes, and warm the taco shells or tortillas according to package directions.
Set everything out and let everyone build their own tacos with beef, cheddar, lettuce, tomato, sour cream, and salsa.

meals.name: Creamy Garlic Chicken Pasta
meals.description: A weeknight favorite that cooks in a single pot: seared chicken and penne simmered in a creamy garlic-parmesan sauce, finished with spinach. Total cook time is approximately 35 minutes.
instructions: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces and season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large pot or deep skillet over medium-high heat and cook the chicken 5-6 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Melt the butter, add the minced garlic, and cook 30 seconds. Pour in the chicken broth and Italian seasoning and bring to a simmer.
Add the penne, stir, cover, and simmer 12-14 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the pasta is tender and most of the liquid is absorbed.
Stir in the heavy cream and parmesan until creamy. Return the chicken, add the spinach, and stir until the spinach wilts and everything is heated through. Serve.

meals.name: Baked Sausage, Peppers & Potatoes
meals.description: A hands-off oven dinner: smoked sausage, potatoes, peppers, and onion all roasted together on one pan until browned and tender. Throw it in and relax. Total cook time is approximately 45 minutes.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C) and line a large sheet pan with foil or parchment. Halve the baby potatoes, slice the sausage into coins, and cut the pepper and onion into chunks.
Pile everything on the sheet pan. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with Italian seasoning, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper. Toss to coat and spread in a single layer.
Roast for 30-35 minutes, tossing once halfway through, until the potatoes are fork-tender and the sausage is browned at the edges. Serve hot.

meals.name: Beef & Broccoli Stir-Fry
meals.description: Faster and tastier than takeout: tender beef and crisp broccoli in a glossy garlic-ginger sauce. Serve over rice. Total cook time is approximately 30 minutes.
instructions: Cook white rice according to package directions. Thinly slice the steak against the grain. In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, brown sugar, corn starch, sesame oil, minced garlic, grated ginger, and 1/3 cup water to make the sauce.
Heat 1 tablespoon vegetable oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add the beef in a single layer and sear 2-3 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Add the remaining oil and the broccoli with a splash of water. Stir-fry 4-5 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Return the beef, pour in the sauce, and toss 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats everything. Serve over the rice.

meals.name: Smothered Pork Chops & Gravy
meals.description: Comfort food, fast: pan-seared pork chops smothered in a rich onion gravy. Great over rice. Total cook time is approximately 35 minutes.
instructions: Cook white rice according to package directions. Pat the pork chops dry and season both sides with garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper, then lightly dredge in about half the flour.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the pork chops 3-4 minutes per side until golden. Remove and set aside.

Add the butter and thinly sliced onion to the skillet and cook 4-5 minutes until softened. Sprinkle in the remaining flour and stir 1 minute, then slowly whisk in the chicken broth until smooth and thickened.
Return the pork chops to the skillet, spoon gravy over them, reduce heat to low, and simmer 8-10 minutes until cooked through (145°F). Serve over the rice with plenty of gravy.

meals.name: Sheet Pan Shrimp Fajitas
meals.description: Quick and easy sheet pan fajitas with juicy shrimp, colorful bell peppers, and onions, seasoned to perfection and served with warm tortillas and fresh lime. Total cook time is approximately 45 minutes.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large sheet pan with parchment paper. Slice bell peppers and onion into thin strips.

In a large bowl, combine the sliced bell peppers and onion with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, 1 tsp chili powder, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp paprika, and 1/4 tsp oregano. Toss to coat evenly. Spread vegetables in a single layer on the prepared sheet pan.

Roast the vegetables for 15 minutes. While vegetables are roasting, pat the shrimp dry. In the same bowl, combine the shrimp with the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil, 1/2 tsp chili powder, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp paprika, and 1/4 tsp oregano. Toss to coat.

After 15 minutes, remove the sheet pan from the oven. Add the seasoned shrimp to the sheet pan, tossing gently with the vegetables. Return to the oven and roast for another 8-10 minutes, or until the shrimp are pink and cooked through and the vegetables are tender. Warm tortillas according to package directions.
Serve the shrimp and vegetable mixture immediately with warm tortillas and lime wedges for squeezing.
